---
title: "Gradient Forest - Eucalyptus obliqua"
author: "Simon E. Lobos"
date: "June, 20267"
output: html_document
---

Import packages
```{r include = TRUE, message=FALSE, eval = FALSE}
#Code adapted from: Sallé et al. (2019). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-019-12695-4
#                   DeSaix et al. (2022). https://doi.org/10.1111/ddi.13628

setwd("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/EO")
 
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(gradientForest)
library(ggplot2)
```

Prepare input to make population level allele frequencies
```{r }
#In an excel spreedsheet combined BayPass (EO_outliers_BF20_gm012.csv) and LFMM2 (EO_outliers_lfmm2.csv) results. Get only unique snps and save it as a txt file in the working directory (EO_outliers_BP_LF.txt).

#read genetic matrix in 012 format
x = read.table("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_1_BayPass/EO/Core/EO_012.txt", check.names=FALSE)

uniq_snpss = read.table("EO_outliers_BP_LF.txt")

#remove duplicate detections, if any
uniq_snpss <- uniq_snpss[!duplicated(uniq_snpss$V1),] 

x_t <- data.frame(t(x)); dim(x_t)

#x_t <- data.frame(x_t, row.names = paste0(1:2521)); dim(x_t)

sig_df_1 <- x_t[uniq_snpss,]; dim(sig_df_1)

rownames(sig_df_1) <- gsub("/", "_", rownames(sig_df_1))
rownames(sig_df_1) <- gsub("-", "_", rownames(sig_df_1))

write.csv(sig_df_1, file=paste0("EO_outliers_BF_LF_gm012.csv"))

sig_df_1[1:10,1:10]
```

Calculate population level allele frequencies
```{r }
#Upload Env input with rows and columns names
Env<-read.csv("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/2_Environmental_data/EO_Env_lfmm_2.csv", header=T)
head(Env)

#get allele frequency for each population 
library(tidyr)

euc.af <-  apply(t(sig_df_1), 2, function(snp) {
  split(snp, Env$pop) %>%
    sapply(function(genos) {
      n = sum(!is.na(genos)) * 2
      s = sum(genos, na.rm=T)
      return (s / n)
    })
})

#check if there are any NAs and convert the NAs to 0
which(apply(euc.af, 2, function(x) any(is.na(x)))) 

euc.af <- euc.af[ , colSums(is.na(euc.af)) == 0]

write.csv(euc.af, file="euc.af.csv")
```

Run Gradient Forest
```{r }
###Environmental file aggregate
agg_Env <- aggregate(. ~ pop, data = Env[,-1], FUN = mean)
all_names <- colnames(Env)
sel_bio <- all_names[5:8] 

EnvGF.euc <- agg_Env[,sel_bio]

maxLevel <- log2(0.368*nrow(EnvGF.euc)/2) #account for correlations, see ?gradientForest 

colnames(euc.af) <- paste0("V", colnames(euc.af))

euc.BP.lfmm.GF <- gradientForest(cbind(EnvGF.euc, #Environmental variables
                                euc.af), #allele freq arranged by site from Env
                          predictor.vars=colnames(EnvGF.euc),
                          response.vars=colnames(euc.af), 
                          ntree=500, maxLevel=maxLevel, 
                          trace=T, corr.threshold=0.50)

summary(euc.BP.lfmm.GF)
```

R square (min,max,mean)
```{r }
sqar <- euc.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq
sqar[sqar == 0] <- NA
sqar[1:4,1:17]

max(sqar,na.rm = T)

min(sqar,na.rm = T)

mean(sqar,na.rm = T)

#Get R2 variable importance (per Env var)
eo.R2.imp <- as.data.frame(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq)
write.table(eo.R2.imp, file="eo.R2.imp.txt", sep="\t", quote = FALSE)

#Get R2 per allele
eo.R2perspp <- as.data.frame(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$result)
write.table(eo.R2perspp, file="eo.R2perspp.txt", sep="\t", quote = FALSE)

## Here need to extract importance numbers to plot separately (as heatmap):
eo.cand.imp <- as.data.frame(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$overall.imp)
eo.cand.r2<- as.data.frame(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$overall.imp2)
```

Overall Importance of Environmental Variables plot
```{r }
png("EO_overall_importance_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
o <- plot(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, plot.type = "O")
dev.off()

most_important <- names(importance(euc.BP.lfmm.GF))

#Overallimportance in ggplot2 
importance(euc.BP.lfmm.GF)

euc.BP.lfmm.GF=c("0.05780571", "0.05696528", "0.04452368", "0.04240527")
names(euc.BP.lfmm.GF)=c("TWI","pHc","BIO05","AVP")
i = data.frame(euc.BP.lfmm.GF2)
i$var = factor(rownames(i))
i$type = 'BioClim'
i$type[grep('pHc',i$var)] = 'Soil'
i$type[grep('AVP',i$var)] = 'Soil'
i$type[grep('TWI',i$var)] = 'Hydrology'
i$var = factor(i$var,levels=i$var[order(i[,1])])

png(file='EO_overall_importance_ggplot_BP_LF.png', width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
ggplot(i,aes(x=var,y=i[,1],fill = type)) +
  coord_flip() + xlab('Climatic variable') + ylab('Proportion of variance explained') +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#CC79A7","#7DCCFF","#43BA8F")) +
  theme(legend.position='none',axis.title = element_text(size=16),axis.text= element_text(size=14) ) +
  geom_bar(stat='identity')
dev.off()
```

Split importance plot
```{r }
png("EO_splits_density_plot_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
s <- plot(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, plot.type = "S", imp.vars = most_important,
leg.posn = "topright", cex.legend = 0.4, cex.axis = 0.6,
cex.lab = 0.7, line.ylab = 0.9, par.args = list(mgp = c(1.5,
0.5, 0), mar = c(3.1, 1.5, 0.1, 1)))
dev.off()
```

Species Cumulative importance plot
```{r }
png("EO_species_cumulative_plot_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
c <- plot(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, plot.type = "C", imp.vars = most_important,common.scale=T,
show.overall = F, legend = T, leg.posn = "topleft",
leg.nspecies = 5, cex.lab = 0.7, cex.legend = 0.4,
cex.axis = 0.6, line.ylab = 0.9, par.args = list(mgp = c(1.5,
0.5, 0), mar = c(2.5, 1, 0.1, 0.5), omi = c(0,
0.3, 0, 0)))
dev.off()
```

Predictor Cumulative importance plot
```{r }
png("EO_predictor_cumulative_plot_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
plot(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, plot.type = "C", imp.vars = most_important,
show.species = F, common.scale = T, cex.axis = 0.6,
cex.lab = 0.7, line.ylab = 0.9, par.args = list(mgp = c(1.5,
0.5, 0), mar = c(2.5, 1, 0.1, 0.5), omi = c(0,
0.3, 0, 0)))
dev.off()

##Predictor cumulative plot - ggplot
preds <- names(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$overall.imp)
cand.preds.list <- list()
for(i in preds){
  CU <- cumimp(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, i)
  cand.preds.list[[i]] <- data.frame(x = CU$x,
                                     y = CU$y,
                                     pred = i,
                                     snps = "eo.cand")
}
eo.cand.preds.df <- do.call(rbind, cand.preds.list)
save(eo.cand.preds.df, file = "eo.cand.preds.df.RData")# Save object to combine cumulative importance for each spp and plot them, then go to next script: "Combined_GF_BP_LF.Rmd".
#cand.preds.df$type <- "mean"

png("EO_predictor_cumulative_ggplot_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
ggplot() +
  geom_step(data=eo.cand.preds.df, aes(x=x,y=y), linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~pred, scale=c("free")) +
  ylab("Cumulative Importance")+
  theme_light() 
dev.off()
```

Species performance rank plot
```{r }
png("EO_species_performance_rank_BP_LF.png", width=1500, height=1200, res=200)
p <- plot(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, plot.type = "P", show.names = F, horizontal = F,
cex.axis = 1, cex.labels = 0.7, line = 2.5)
dev.off()

save(euc.BP.lfmm.GF, file = "EO_GF.RData")
```
